prd.oneLiner: Manage decision records, alternative comparisons, impact analysis, reviews, approvals, exceptions, updates, and connected technical evidence.
prd.goal: Achieve 95% or more documented rationale coverage for critical architecture decisions and reduce re-review time.
prd.whyNow: Design alternatives, rationale, approvals, and downstream impact are scattered across meeting notes and code reviews, so teams repeat debates and miss risk during change.
prd.problem: Design alternatives, rationale, approvals, and downstream impact are scattered across meeting notes and code reviews, so teams repeat debates and miss risk during change.
prd.solution: Manage decision records, alternative comparisons, impact analysis, reviews, approvals, exceptions, updates, and connected technical evidence.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: Development and architecture team reviewing technical design and change
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Design alternatives, rationale, approvals, and downstream impact are scattered across meeting notes and code reviews, so teams repeat debates and miss risk during change.

targets.name: Software architecture and technical-governance coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Design alternatives, rationale, approvals, and downstream impact are scattered across meeting notes and code reviews, so teams repeat debates and miss risk during change.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Design alternatives, rationale, approvals, and downstream impact are scattered across meeting notes and code reviews, so teams repeat debates and miss risk during change.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Achieve 95% or more documented rationale coverage for critical architecture decisions and reduce re-review time.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Architecture decision, alternative, and impact-analysis registration
Decision status, rationale, dependency, and change-history lookup
Technical review, approval, exception handling, decision update, and retirement
Decision lead time, exceptions, technical debt, and impact-scope reporting
Architecture decision, alternative, and impact-analysis registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
